title: Topic, Headers, Alternate, and Exchange-to-Exchange Routing · Compiler Case: Topic Segment Design
summary: Routing keys encode unstable JSON, topic wildcards overlap unexpectedly, headers matching trusts absent fields, and unroutable messages vanish because mandatory and alternate behavior are not designed together. Planned artifact: Add and verify the topic segment design increment in multi-strategy routing and unroutable-message table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching.
State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Topic Segment Design · Theory Topic Segment Design
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6691 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching.
